summary: CISA AA24-109A (updated Nov. 13, 2025) StopRansomware advisory for Akira ransomware (also known as Storm-1567, Howling Scorpius, Punk Spider, Gold Sahara). Active since March 2023, targeting SMBs and critical infrastructure across North America, Europe, and Australia. Primary sectors: Education, Critical Manufacturing, IT, Healthcare, Financial Services, Food and Agriculture. Initial access via VPN exploitation without MFA (CVE-2020-3259, CVE-2023-20269), phishing, and valid credentials. Uses hybrid ChaCha20+RSA encryption; variants include Windows Akira (.akira), Megazord (.powerranges), Akirav2 (.akiranew/.aki), and Linux ESXi encryptor. June 2025 update: Nutanix AHV targeting via CVE-2024-40766 (SonicWall). As of late September 2025, ~$244.17M in ransomware proceeds claimed. Double extortion model with Bitcoin payments. No IoPs left on victim networks until contacted.

timeline.event: Akira ransomware first active, initial focus on Windows systems
timeline.evidence_snippet: Since March 2023, Akira ransomware threat actors have impacted a wide range of businesses

timeline.event: Akira threat actors deployed Linux variant targeting VMware ESXi virtual machines
timeline.evidence_snippet: In April 2023, following an initial focus on Windows systems, Akira threat actors deployed a Linux variant targeting VMware ESXi VMs
beginning in August 2023, some Akira attacks began deploying a Megazord encryptor

timeline.event: Akira ransomware samples in Table 6 created
timeline.evidence_snippet: trusted third-party analysis confirmed the Akira threat actors ransomware samples in Table 6 were created on Dec. 28, 2023

timeline.event: Akira threat actors deployed payloads targeting Nutanix AHV systems via CVE-2024-40766
timeline.evidence_snippet: Based on trusted third-party reporting, Akira threat actors deployed payloads to target Nutanix AHV systems in a June 2025 incident

timeline.event: Akira ransomware claimed approximately $244.17M USD in ransomware proceeds
timeline.evidence_snippet: As of late September 2025, Akira ransomware has claimed approximately 244.17 million USD

timeline.event: CISA AA24-109A advisory updated with new TTPs, IOCs, and imminent critical infrastructure threat
timeline.evidence_snippet: November 13, 2025: Updated to share recent TTPs and IOCs
